svg.text: Step 1 — assume these are ALL the primes
← suppose this is the complete list
Step 2 — multiply them all together, then add 1
Step 3 — try dividing N by every prime on the list
Every prime on the list leaves remainder 1 — because N was built by adding 1 to their product.
Step 4 — N must be prime or composite. Either way: contradiction.

g.text: 211 is prime — not on the list!
its prime factor is also not on the list

svg.text: In this example 211 is actually prime — Branch A applies directly. The new prime not on the list is 211 itself.
∴ No finite list can contain all primes — the primes are infinite.
